universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Regime-filtered SPY/QQQ put credit spreads with legacy fill assumptions. Best overall performer: +139% return, 98.6% win rate, 3.87 Sharpe, only 2.1% max drawdown over 2020-2025.
hypothesis: High hit-rate premium collection via short OTM puts on broad-market ETFs during bullish regimes.
entry_rules: Price above MA200 and MA20 above MA50 (bullish regime)
Short put 4.5% OTM, width 5% of price
Risk 1.5% of capital per trade, max 3 contracts
management_rules: Take profit at 50% of credit received (min 3 days held)
Stop at 2.2x credit received or 55% of width
Close at 10 DTE (time exit)
Close if long strike breached

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Legacy PCS + HV30 gate: skips entries when SPY HV30 < 10% (too calm, thin premium) or > 40% (panic). Result: +125.8% return, 98.45% win rate, PF 21.51 — slightly lower than legacy_replica (+139%) due to missed entries in extreme-vol periods.
hypothesis: Moderate volatility (10-40%) produces the most favourable risk/reward for short put credit spreads.

universe_note: QQQ only - daily-rule proxy for a high-probability income spread entered after sharp downside moves in elevated volatility.
description: QQQ-only put credit spread profile inspired by a tastylive-style falling-knife income trade. Uses long-term trend support plus short-term selloff, RSI, and pullback filters to define when a high-POP bullish spread is allowed.
hypothesis: A far-OTM QQQ put credit spread sold only after sharp, high-volatility pullbacks can monetize panic premium while keeping buying-power use small and defined risk.
entry_rules: Price above MA200; MA20 above MA50 is not required
Short put 6.0% OTM, width 4.0% of price
Credit target about 18% of spread width
management_rules: Take profit at 50% of credit after at least 2 days
Stop at 1.9x credit or 55% of width
Force close at 7 DTE
Close if long strike is breached

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Trend-filtered PCS baseline with volatility-target sizing (18% annual target) and symbol notional caps.
hypothesis: Regime confirmation plus vol-target sizing should improve risk-adjusted consistency versus static-size PCS.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Defensive trend-filtered PCS with lower target volatility (14%), tighter risk budget, and reduced symbol notional cap.
hypothesis: Defensive vol-target sizing should reduce drawdowns while preserving a high win-rate profile.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with synthetic/observed data-quality flags
description: Open-at-09:35 and close-by-15:55 intraday option strategy using ITM, ATR, unusual-flow proxy, and similarity win-rate scoring.
hypothesis: Balanced target/stop profile can capture intraday directional moves while limiting downside with fixed same-day exits.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with stricter spread and unusual-flow thresholds
description: Conservative intraday profile with tighter stop-loss and earlier trailing activation to improve consistency and win rate.
hypothesis: Lower risk-per-trade and stricter quality gates should improve win-rate at the cost of capped upside.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with looser spread and opportunity filters
description: Aggressive intraday profile with larger target and wider stop to pursue larger single-session option gains.
hypothesis: Higher reward-to-risk profile can outperform in strong directional sessions, with lower win-rate tolerance.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with moderately tighter flow and liquidity gates
description: Middle-ground intraday variant between baseline and oos_hardened. Raises liquidity score threshold (0.35 -> 0.42) and unusual-flow gate (1.05x -> 1.10x) while keeping 3 trades/day and 2% allocation to target better OOS stability without sacrificing trade count.
hypothesis: The baseline's 2024-2025 OOS collapse (from 6496% IS to near-zero) is driven by weak-quality setups that pass a 0.35 liquidity score but fail live. Tightening just 2 filters should recover OOS Sharpe while keeping enough trades to be statistically meaningful.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with the conservative baseline plus light regime alignment
description: Conservative baseline plus symbol regime alignment only. Keeps modeled data allowed and sizing unchanged so the test isolates whether light trend filtering improves stability without starving trade count.
hypothesis: A light regime gate should remove the worst countertrend entries while preserving enough trades to keep the edge measurable.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with conservative baseline thresholds plus historical edge confirmation
description: Conservative baseline plus a minimum 55% historical bucket win rate with at least 6 observations. Tests whether similarity-confirmed setups improve selectivity without the zero-trade problem of the hardened variants.
hypothesis: Requiring a modest historical edge floor should filter weak pattern matches and improve consistency.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with tighter spread, liquidity, and unusual-flow scanning criteria
description: Conservative baseline with tighter scan-quality thresholds and slightly smaller per-trade sizing. Focuses on execution quality instead of hard walk-forward exclusions.
hypothesis: Better scan quality and a small sizing trim should improve drawdown stability more efficiently than the zero-trade hardened profiles.

universe_note: Uses selected universe profile (default top_50) with strict liquidity, spread, and data-quality gates
description: Validation-focused intraday profile: one high-quality trade/day max, tighter stops, smaller allocation, stricter liquidity thresholds, and stronger modeled-data penalties.
hypothesis: Reducing trade count and tightening execution-quality filters should improve out-of-sample Sharpe and drawdown stability, even with lower raw return.

universe_note: Top-50 universe with entry-safe lagged feature timing and modeled-only candidates excluded
description: Validation sweep v1 with low turnover and strict liquidity constraints.
hypothesis: Better fill quality and lower noise should improve OOS consistency versus baseline intraday profiles.

universe_note: Top-50 universe with entry-safe lagged features, stronger unusual-flow threshold, and modeled-only exclusion
description: Validation sweep v2 emphasizing unusual-flow quality and tighter spread controls.
hypothesis: Higher conviction flow setups can improve Sharpe even with fewer total trades.

universe_note: Top-50 universe with lagged symbol trend plus SPY regime alignment
description: Validation sweep v3 using strict directional regime gating for both symbol and index context.
hypothesis: Filtering entries to aligned market regimes reduces adverse directional noise.

universe_note: Top-50 universe candidate intended for validation promotion if OOS criteria are met
description: Validation sweep v4 blended profile targeting pass criteria under 504/126/126 walk-forward.
hypothesis: Balanced gating can retain enough edge while keeping drawdown and variability controlled.

universe_note: Best results with focused default universe (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL, META, TSLA, SPY, QQQ, AMD). Top_50 dilutes performance with lower-quality symbols.
description: Deep ITM call replacement at ~80 delta. No trend gate. Flexible DTE (8-60 days). +45.7% return with top_50 universe, higher with focused default universe.
hypothesis: Balanced directional exposure with high delta capturing most of the underlying move.
management_rules: Roll when delta falls below 0.65
Roll when DTE <= 7
Keep one position per underlying symbol

universe_note: Best with focused default universe. Sector gate requires XLK/XLC/XLY in cache.
description: All four gates stacked: VIX proxy + breadth (market-wide) + order block + sector trend (per-symbol). Result: +71.1%, Sharpe 0.29, max DD 36.9% — best stock replacement result. Synergistic effect: market gates filter bad conditions, per-symbol filters select best entries within good conditions.
hypothesis: Stacking all market-quality filters maximises signal precision at the cost of trade frequency and raw return.

universe_note: Most faithful replication of Chris Uhl's complete OVTLYR strategy
description: Complete OVTLYR ruleset: 80 delta monthly calls, 30-90 DTE, sit-in-cash regime gate, plus Plan M exits — close on bearish EMA cross, take profit at +30%, stop loss at -20%. These Plan M exits were missing from all other variants.
hypothesis: Adding Plan M exits (bearish cross close, profit target, stop loss) should improve Sharpe by cutting losers and locking gains, closely matching Chris Uhl's actual trading discipline.

universe_note: Same universe as uhl_authentic — trend gates drive stock selection more than universe size
description: Uhl rules (monthly calls, trend gate, sit-in-cash) with ONLY the bearish EMA cross exit — no profit target or stop loss. Tests whether the -20% SL was causing the -37% return in uhl_authentic by exiting too early on volatile tech stocks.
hypothesis: Removing the stop loss and profit target should allow winners to run longer and avoid premature SL exits on volatile tech options.

universe_note: Same universe as uhl_authentic — trend gates drive stock selection more than universe size
description: Uhl rules + Plan M exits but with -40% stop loss instead of -20%. Deep ITM calls on volatile tech stocks need more room before cutting — a tighter stop causes frequent early exits before the position recovers.
hypothesis: Widening the stop loss from -20% to -40% reduces premature exits while still protecting against genuine trend reversals.

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. Raising max_positions 10→20 turned +71.1% into +323.8% — 4x uplift from diversification alone.
description: full_filter_stack (all 4 signal gates) with max_positions raised from 10 to 20 and top_50 universe. Massive improvement from diversification: +323.8% return, Sharpe 0.65, Max DD 56.4%. Baseline champion for stock-selection experiments.
hypothesis: More concurrent positions in a filtered universe reduces concentration risk and improves overall return without sacrificing quality.

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. IV rank gate cuts max drawdown from 56.4%→41.4% with only 21pp return sacrifice. Best Sharpe in the stock replacement family.
description: full_filter_20pos + IV rank gate: only enters when a symbol options IV rank is in the bottom 40th percentile of its own 252-day history (cheap options). Result: +302.7%, Sharpe 0.70, Max DD 41.4%. Lower return than baseline but significantly better risk profile (DD -15pp, Sharpe +0.05).
hypothesis: Buying deep ITM calls when IV is historically low reduces the extrinsic cost basis and improves per-trade risk/reward.

strategy_name: Full Filter IV Rank Trend Liquid
universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. Keeps the full_filter_iv_rank gate stack, then adds symbol-trend and tighter option-quality constraints to target lower OOS drawdown.
description: full_filter_iv_rank tightened with higher target delta, lower extrinsic cap, tighter spread limits, shorter 21-45 DTE window, and bearish-cross exits.
hypothesis: Pairing the existing IV-rank entry edge with stricter liquidity/theta filters and trend exits should improve walk-forward drawdown and Sharpe, even if raw return falls.

universe_note: top_50 universe — RS requires SPY in the cache as benchmark
description: full_filter_20pos + relative strength gate: only enters when a stock has outperformed SPY over the past 90 trading days. RS avoids the crash-survivor failure mode of absolute momentum. Result: +208.2%, Sharpe 0.53, Max DD 52.5%. Worse than baseline — RS alone over-filters.
hypothesis: Stocks outperforming SPY have genuine relative strength and are more likely to continue their directional trend, improving win rate for directional call buyers.

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. Best risk-adjusted variant: +320.5% return (near baseline) with 15.6pp lower max drawdown (40.8% vs 56.4%).
description: full_filter_20pos + both IV rank (< 40th percentile, 252d lookback) AND relative strength (stock >= SPY over 90d) gates combined. Best risk-adjusted result: +320.5% (near baseline), Sharpe 0.68, Max DD 40.8% (vs 56.4% baseline). Nearly same return with 15.6pp lower drawdown.
hypothesis: Combining cheap option premium with relative outperformance captures the best risk/reward setups: strong stock + inexpensive calls.

strategy_name: Full Filter IV RS Trend Liquid
universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. Keeps the IV-rank + relative-strength stack, then adds symbol-trend and tighter option-quality constraints to target lower OOS drawdown.
description: full_filter_iv_rs tightened with higher target delta, lower extrinsic cap, tighter spread limits, shorter 21-45 DTE window, and bearish-cross exits.
hypothesis: Combining cheap premium, relative outperformance, cleaner execution filters, and trend exits should improve OOS stability versus the existing full_filter_iv_rs baseline.

universe_note: top_50 universe â€” momentum ranking requires enough stocks to compare
description: full_filter_20pos + 90-day momentum rank gate (top 50% by return). Result: +29.6%, Max DD 11.2% — momentum filter hurts returns by blocking recovery entries after market drawdowns.
hypothesis: Stock-level momentum ranking above the 4 quality gates should concentrate entries in the strongest performers.

universe_note: top_50 universe
description: full_filter_20pos + 90-day momentum rank gate (top 25% only). Result: +7.2%, Max DD 6.3% — too few qualifying entries, compounding effect lost.
hypothesis: Only entering the top-quartile momentum stocks should produce the cleanest directional exposure.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Larger risk budget (1.5%), better credit (30% of width), tighter stop (1.9x), broader IV window. +58.6% return, 100% win rate, 1.58 Sharpe, 0.57% max drawdown.
hypothesis: Improved return over realistic_priced while maintaining near-100% win rate.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Higher turnover with faster profit-taking (40% target) and broader IV window (up to 65%). +54.1% return, 100% win rate, 1.37 Sharpe.
hypothesis: Higher annualized return through more frequent entry/exit cycles in moderate-volatility regimes.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Conservative sizing (1.0% risk), early winner exits (35% profit target), closes at 14 DTE. +51.3% return, 100% win rate, 1.26 Sharpe, 0.58% max drawdown.
hypothesis: Smoother equity curve with lowest drawdown at the cost of slightly lower upside.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Stricter fills, tighter volatility window (18-55%), higher fees ($4/contract), max 2 contracts. +40.6% return, 98.3% win rate, 0.47 Sharpe, 0.80% max drawdown.
hypothesis: Most conservative real-world estimate of put credit spread profitability.

universe_note: Best results with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Higher-delta variant (0.85 target, +/-0.08) for more stock-like behavior. +40.3% return with top_50 universe.
hypothesis: Lower gamma/theta sensitivity with tighter delta range, at the cost of higher premium outlay.

universe_note: Best results with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Limits entries to contracts with extrinsic <= 20% of ask price. +38.0% return with top_50 universe.
hypothesis: Improved theta efficiency per dollar deployed; accepts fewer but cleaner entries.

universe_note: Best results with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Lower-delta variant (0.70 target) for increased leverage per dollar. +29.3% return with top_50 universe.
hypothesis: Higher potential upside in strong trends but weaker consistency in choppy markets.

universe_note: Best results with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Restricts all entries and rolls to monthly (3rd Friday) expirations only, 30-60 DTE. +26.1% return with top_50 universe.
hypothesis: Improved fills and liquidity via standardised monthly strikes; fewer but higher-quality entries.

universe_note: Trend gates override stock selection — universe choice has minimal impact on this variant.
description: Full Chris Uhl / OVTLYR University ruleset: monthly-only, 30-90 DTE (fixed from broken 30-60), 10/20/50 EMA trend gate, sit-in-cash when SPY bearish. Previously showed -102.7% due to DTE bug — now corrected.
hypothesis: DTE window fix resolves entry desert caused by monthly_only + 30-60 DTE combination.

universe_note: Best tested with top_50 — more stocks means more qualifying entries under strict filters
description: Complete Uhl strategy (Plan M exits + trend gates) combined with all 4 signal gates (VIX < 28%, breadth > 50%, order block, sector ETF trend). Most selective variant — only enters in ideal market conditions AND exits on trend reversal/targets.

universe_note: Performance improves significantly with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps) vs top_50.
description: Deep ITM call stock replacement using legacy OpenClaw parameters (0.85 delta target, 35% extrinsic max, 20-75 DTE). No trend gate. +47.1% return.
hypothesis: Higher-delta entry and wider DTE window may improve opportunity count versus base profile.

universe_note: Performance improves with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps) vs top_50.
description: OpenClaw stock-options with tighter fills (0.82 delta, 25% extrinsic max, 8% spread max, 25-60 DTE) and explicit cost overlay.
hypothesis: Stricter parameters closer to live execution reduce over-optimism in backtests.

universe_note: Trades TQQQ (or synthetic 3x QQQ) only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: Pullback-to-MA swing trades on TQQQ (or 3x synthetic from QQQ) under legacy assumptions. +13.7% return over 2020-2025.
hypothesis: Momentum reversion in leveraged ETFs can generate outsized returns during trend periods.

universe_note: Trades TQQQ (or synthetic 3x QQQ) only — not affected by stock universe selection
description: TQQQ swing strategy with tighter risk filters (10% stop, 25% target) and higher friction. -2.4% return over 2020-2025.
hypothesis: Realistic friction assumptions expose the sensitivity of leveraged ETF swing timing.

universe_note: Stock leg improves with focused default universe; TQQQ leg unaffected.
description: Blended engine: 75% stock-options leg + 25% TQQQ swing leg, legacy assumptions. +38.8% return.
hypothesis: Diversified signal sources reduce single-strategy concentration risk.

universe_note: Stock leg improves with focused default universe; TQQQ leg unaffected.
description: Blended 75/25 stock-options + TQQQ swing with realistic execution friction on both legs.
hypothesis: Realistic blended returns; both legs face friction headwinds simultaneously.

universe_note: Best with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Base strategy + VIX proxy gate: skips entries when SPY HV30 > 25% (panic). Result: -54.6% return — gate is too restrictive, blocking profitable 2020-2021 recovery entries.
hypothesis: Avoiding high-volatility / panic regimes reduces drawdowns for deep-ITM call buyers.

universe_note: Best with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Base + strict breadth gate (< 60% above MA200 = no entries). Result: -19.5%, 361 cash days. Too restrictive — blocked too many entries in 2020-2021.
hypothesis: Trading only when most stocks are in uptrends should improve win rate and reduce 2022-style drawdowns.

universe_note: Best with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Base + permissive breadth gate (< 40% above MA200 = no entries). Result: -2.3% — still too restrictive, 221 cash days. Breadth alone insufficient for deep-ITM calls.
hypothesis: A looser breadth filter maintains more trade frequency while still avoiding the weakest market conditions.

universe_note: Best with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: Base + order block resistance gate: skips entries when price is within 3% below 30-day high. Result: +45.7% (identical to base) — large-caps in uptrends are always near highs, so gate rarely fires.
hypothesis: Avoiding entries near recent highs reduces the chance of being caught in a resistance rejection and forced into a loss.

universe_note: Best with focused default universe. Requires XLK/XLC/XLY sector ETFs in the Parquet cache — if absent, gate is inactive.
description: Base + sector ETF trend gate (XLK/XLC/XLY must be bullish). Result: +36.5% — slightly below base. Note: sector ETFs must be in Parquet cache for gate to activate.
hypothesis: Sector-level trend alignment provides a stronger regime filter than the individual stock trend alone.

universe_note: Best with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Top_50 dilutes performance.
description: VIX proxy (HV30 < 28%) + breadth (> 50% above MA200) combined market gates. Result: +2.5%, 305 cash days. Gates block the right periods but per-symbol selection still neutral.
hypothesis: Combining two independent market-health signals should produce the best risk-adjusted returns of all stock replacement variants.

universe_note: Trades SPY only - not affected by stock universe selection
description: Monthly cash-secured put-writing prototype with trend and volatility gates.
hypothesis: Systematic put-selling in favorable regimes can outperform long-only with controlled drawdown.

universe_note: Trades SPY only - not affected by stock universe selection
description: Lower-allocation defensive putwrite variant with tighter volatility window.
hypothesis: Reducing notional and requiring cleaner regimes improves risk-adjusted returns.

universe_note: Trades SPY only - not affected by stock universe selection
description: Monthly covered-call overwrite prototype with trend and volatility gates.
hypothesis: Covered-call premium harvesting in trending regimes improves return consistency.

universe_note: Trades SPY only - not affected by stock universe selection
description: Defensive buywrite variant with lower premium assumptions and stricter entry conditions.
hypothesis: Lower-yield but more selective covered-call entries may reduce large drawdowns.

universe_note: Trades SPY only - not affected by stock universe selection
description: Monthly collar prototype: long SPY + long put - short call.
hypothesis: Collars can preserve upside participation while materially reducing downside tails.

universe_note: Trades SPY only - not affected by stock universe selection
description: Defensive collar variant with wider downside buffer.
hypothesis: Paying more for protection improves survivability in stress regimes.

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. SPY regime block added on top of IV rank + RS gates — no new entries when SPY 10 EMA < 20 EMA.
description: +162.4%, Sharpe 0.53, Max DD 70.6%. FAILED: SPY regime block increased drawdown vs full_filter_iv_rs (40.8%) — blocking new entries while existing positions bleed through 2022 bear market. Same failure mode as uhl_authentic.
hypothesis: IV rank + RS already filter low-quality entries. Adding SPY regime block prevents opening positions into sustained bear markets, directly targeting the 2022 drawdown source.

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. 30% profit target added to champion full_filter_20pos.
description: +109.7%, Sharpe 0.55, Max DD 78.4%. FAILED: 30% profit target destroyed compounding — deep ITM calls need to run, early exits cut mega-winners that drove full_filter_20pos to +323.8%.
hypothesis: Deep ITM calls up 30% have delivered most intrinsic gain. Re-entering at a cheaper IV may compound better than riding the position to expiry.

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. Stricter IV rank cutoff: 30th percentile vs 40th in full_filter_iv_rank.
description: +149.8%, Sharpe 0.47, Max DD 53.1%. FAILED: 30th percentile IV rank too selective vs 40th — returns halved with no improvement in risk metrics. 40th percentile was the sweet spot.
hypothesis: Stricter IV selectivity finds the very cheapest options historically — fewer but higher-quality entries. Risk: trade count falls below statistical threshold.

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. Monthly-only expirations (3rd Friday, 30-90 DTE) with full 4-gate stack + IV rank.
description: +237.7%, Sharpe 0.58, Max DD 53.3%. Partial failure: best of the new batch but significantly below full_filter_iv_rank (+302.7%). Monthly-only reduces opportunity set without improving quality enough.
hypothesis: Monthly options deliver better fills and lower extrinsic decay early in the DTE window. IV rank gate spaces out entries enough to avoid the monthly_only entry desert problem.

universe_note: top_50 universe. Best of 8 CSP variants â€” still significantly underperforms stock replacement.
description: +35.4% (5yr), Sharpe 0.07, Max DD 21.5%, 87.1% win rate, 1276 trades. Best CSP variant: delta -0.30, 50% profit exit, roll at 7 DTE. The only CSP with marginally positive Sharpe. Despite high win rate, premium collected per contract is tiny relative to locked collateral (~$250 credit on $10k+ collateral = 2.5% max return per trade).
hypothesis: Selling 30-delta puts with DTE-based roll and early profit exits cycles capital more frequently, improving annualized yield vs riding positions to expiry.

universe_note: top_50 universe.
description: +22.5% (5yr), Sharpe -0.05, Max DD 25.4%, 92.4% win rate, 1269 trades. Delta -0.20 target, 50% profit exit, roll on delta cross. High win rate but tiny credits on far OTM puts. Capital efficiency problem: collateral >> premium collected.
hypothesis: Lower delta (safer) puts with profit exit would produce a smoother equity curve with lower drawdown.

universe_note: top_50 universe.
description: +21.9% (5yr), Sharpe -0.08, Max DD 19.0%, 90.2% win rate, 1404 trades. Lowest max drawdown of all CSP variants but still well below stock replacement. Same capital efficiency problem.
hypothesis: DTE-based rolls on OTM puts (lower delta) with profit exits produce the most controlled risk profile of the CSP family.

universe_note: top_50 universe.
description: +21.8% (5yr), Sharpe -0.05, Max DD 25.5%, 89.6% win rate, 1040 trades. Delta -0.30, 50% profit exit, roll on delta cross. Second-worst of delta-roll variants.
hypothesis: Higher credit at 30 delta with profit exit and delta-based roll captures best of both approaches.

universe_note: top_50 universe.
description: +20.3% (5yr), Sharpe -0.07, Max DD 24.8%, 82.7% win rate, 730 trades. Riding to expiry without profit target reduces trade frequency and produces lower win rate (put occasionally goes ITM).
hypothesis: Riding puts to expiry maximizes theta decay capture â€” no exit at 50% means keeping the remaining 50% of premium.

universe_note: top_50 universe.
description: +19.1% (5yr), Sharpe -0.11, Max DD 21.8%, 89.9% win rate, 1072 trades. Lower delta, ride to expiry, delta roll. The 940 rolls executed indicates excessive rolling â€” delta threshold too tight for far OTM puts.
hypothesis: Far OTM puts (delta -0.20) held to expiry consistently expire worthless, producing near-100% win rate with minimal capital risk per trade.

universe_note: top_50 universe.
description: +17.6% (5yr), Sharpe -0.16, Max DD 19.7%, 76.4% win rate, 716 trades. Worst of the ride-to-expiry variants. 707 rolls suggest frequent DTE rollouts with poor compounding.
hypothesis: DTE-based rolling before expiry is safer than holding through final week; should avoid assignment risk and preserve credit.

universe_note: top_50 universe.
description: +7.2% (5yr), Sharpe -0.36, Max DD 17.6%, 80.6% win rate, 718 trades. Worst CSP variant overall. Lower delta + ride + DTE roll = minimal premium collected per cycle + excessive rolling. Even 5yr SPY buy-and-hold outperforms this by ~10x.
hypothesis: Conservative delta + ride-to-expiry should compound smoothly with lowest realized losses.

universe_note: Best with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). LEAPS requires 180-400 DTE â€” monthly expirations only.
description: LEAPS-style deep ITM call at 0.80 delta with 180-400 DTE. Long hold, near-pure intrinsic value tracking, minimal theta decay. 50% profit target, 25% stop loss.
hypothesis: Long-duration deep ITM calls track the underlying stock dollar-for-dollar with minimal extrinsic erosion. Holding for 6-12 months captures large directional moves.
entry_rules: Extrinsic <= 25% of ask (LEAPS have very low extrinsic relative to intrinsic)
Open interest >= 100 (lower threshold â€” LEAPS have thinner markets)
management_rules: Take profit at +50% option value gain
Stop loss at -25% option value loss
Avoid final week of DTE (gamma risk spike)

universe_note: Best with focused default universe (top 10 large-caps). Deeper ITM = less extrinsic, more capital required per contract.
description: LEAPS at 0.85 delta â€” deeper ITM than leaps_80d for near-pure stock tracking. 180-400 DTE, 40% profit target, 20% stop loss. Near-zero extrinsic erodes minimal theta.
hypothesis: 0.85 delta LEAPS are closer to synthetic long stock with leverage. Less gamma exposure, nearly 1:1 dollar tracking, cheapest possible extrinsic cost basis.
entry_rules: Extrinsic <= 15% of ask (stricter â€” deeper ITM should have lower extrinsic ratio)
management_rules: Take profit at +40% option value gain
Stop loss at -20% option value loss

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. IV rank + relative strength gates require sufficient history. SPY must be in cache as benchmark.
description: leaps_80d with all 4 market-quality gates stacked: VIX proxy + breadth + order block + sector trend, PLUS IV rank (< 40th pct) and relative strength vs SPY. Maximum quality filter for LEAPS entries.
hypothesis: Quality gates prevent entering long-duration positions in weak market regimes. Cheap IV + relative outperformance ensures the best LEAPS risk/reward setups.
entry_rules: Price not within 3% of 30-day high (order block gate)
IV rank in bottom 40th percentile (252-day lookback)
Stock outperforming SPY over past 90 days (relative strength)

universe_note: Requires top_50 universe. Most conservative LEAPS variant â€” sits in cash during bear markets.
description: leaps_85d (0.85 delta, deepest ITM) + SPY trend block (no entries when SPY 10 EMA < 20 EMA) + IV rank gate. Sits in cash during bear markets entirely. 40% PT, 20% SL.
hypothesis: LEAPS are expensive long-duration bets. Sitting in cash during SPY downtrends avoids holding a 12-month option through a 20%+ bear market, which would destroy most of the premium paid.
entry_rules: IV rank in bottom 40th percentile (252-day lookback)
No entries when SPY is in a confirmed downtrend

universe_note: Uses top_50 universe. Wheel requires liquid underlying stocks with options for both CSP and covered call phases.
description: Classic wheel strategy: sell -0.30 delta cash-secured put (30-60 DTE) â†’ if assigned, sell 0.30 delta covered call (20-45 DTE) â†’ if called away, repeat. Full CSP-to-covered-call cycle.
hypothesis: The wheel collects premium at every stage: CSP premium + covered call premium. If assigned, cost basis is reduced by both premiums. Mechanical income generation on stocks you'd be willing to own.
entry_rules: Only open new CSP on underlyings not currently wheeling
management_rules: If put assigned (stock price < strike at expiry): buy shares, begin CC phase
Sell covered call at +0.30 delta (20-45 DTE)
If call assigned (stock price > CC strike): sell shares, return to CSP phase
Repeat cycle indefinitely

universe_note: Uses top_50 universe. Same 30-delta cash-secured put entry as the default wheel, but covered calls are sold at 20 delta to leave more upside room after assignment.
description: Targeted wheel refinement: sell the same -0.30 delta cash-secured put as the baseline wheel, then use a lower-delta 0.20 covered call after assignment. The goal is to keep the core premium-selling engine while reducing upside truncation in the covered-call phase.
hypothesis: If the baseline wheel gives up too much rebound upside after assignment, a lower-delta covered call should improve OOS compounding and reduce regret during strong recoveries without changing the CSP entry profile.
entry_rules: Only open new CSP on underlyings not currently wheeling
management_rules: If put assigned (stock price < strike at expiry): buy shares, begin CC phase
Sell covered call at +0.20 delta (20-45 DTE)
If call assigned (stock price > CC strike): sell shares, return to CSP phase
Repeat cycle indefinitely

universe_note: Uses top_50 universe. Lower CSP delta = less assignment probability, longer time in CSP premium-collection phase.
description: Conservative wheel: sell -0.20 delta CSP (lower assignment probability) â†’ if assigned, sell 0.30 delta covered call. Favors premium collection over assignment. Higher win rate on CSPs, slower capital cycling.
hypothesis: Lower put delta reduces assignment frequency. Most premium collected from CSPs that expire worthless. Less stock ownership time = less directional exposure. Safer but lower yield than 0.30 delta.
management_rules: Same as wheel_d30_c30 but assignment less frequent
Covered call at 0.30 delta if assigned

universe_note: Uses top_50 universe. Higher CSP delta = more assignment probability â€” accepts stock frequently.
description: Aggressive wheel: sell -0.40 delta CSP (high assignment probability) â†’ sell 0.30 delta covered call when assigned. Designed to own stock quickly and harvest CC premium. Higher capital utilization but more directional stock exposure.
hypothesis: 0.40 delta puts collect more premium per trade and get assigned more often. More time in the covered call phase means more CC premium harvested. Higher yield but more dependent on stock appreciation not eroding cost basis.
entry_rules: High assignment probability â€” expect to own shares ~40% of the time
management_rules: Same as wheel_d30_c30 but assignment more frequent
Covered call at 0.30 delta when assigned
Must size positions to accommodate frequent stock ownership

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only â€” not affected by stock universe selection
description: Bearish/neutral call credit spread: sell 4.5% OTM call, buy 5% OTM call above that. Collect premium when market stays below short strike. Mirror of PCS but on the upside.
hypothesis: In rangebound or moderately bearish regimes, OTM calls expire worthless. Neutral market conditions produce high win rates similar to OTM put spreads in bullish regimes.
entry_rules: HV20 in 14%-60% range (moderate vol needed for decent premium)
Stock not in strong bullish momentum (< 8% above MA50)
Long call 5% above short strike (defines max loss)
management_rules: Take profit at 50% of credit received (min 3 days held)
Stop at 2.2x credit received or 55% of width
Close at 10 DTE (time exit)
Close if long strike breached (market rallied through full spread)

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only â€” not affected by stock universe selection
description: CCS only when volatility is elevated (HV20 > 18%). Higher vol = more expensive calls = better premium collected. Avoids entering in calm low-vol environments where call premium is thin.
hypothesis: Elevated volatility inflates call premiums. Waiting for HV20 > 18% ensures adequate credit vs width ratio. Quality-over-quantity approach â€” fewer but better-priced entries.
entry_rules: HV20 > 18% (elevated volatility required for good call premium)
Stock not in strong bullish momentum (< 8% above MA50)
management_rules: Same as ccs_baseline

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only â€” not affected by stock universe selection
description: Conservative CCS: short call 6% OTM (lower delta ~15-20), smaller sizing, earlier exit at 12 DTE. Reduces directional risk at the cost of lower premium collected per trade.
hypothesis: Moving the short strike further OTM reduces the probability of breaching the short strike. Lower risk per trade with more room for the market to move against the position.
entry_rules: Short call 6% OTM above spot (lower delta vs baseline)
Smaller position sizing (0.9% risk per trade)
management_rules: Take profit at 45% of credit received
Stop at 2.0x credit received
Close at 12 DTE (earlier time exit than baseline)

universe_note: Trades AAPL only â€” individual stock with ~25-35% IV vs SPY ~15-20%
description: Call credit spread on AAPL. 3.5% OTM short call, 5% wide spread, 35-day DTE. Higher IV than SPY/QQQ generates more premium per trade with manageable idiosyncratic risk.
hypothesis: AAPL IV averages 1.5-2x SPY IV, producing proportionally higher credit. Tighter 3.5% OTM strike manages single-stock event risk while capturing the IV premium.

universe_note: Trades NVDA only â€” high-IV stock with ~50-70% annualized volatility
description: Call credit spread on NVDA. 5% OTM short call, 8% wide spread to absorb NVDA's elevated volatility (3-4x SPY IV). Smaller sizing (0.8% risk/trade) to manage outsized moves.
hypothesis: NVDA IV at 50-70% generates 3-4x SPY premium per trade. Wider strikes and reduced sizing compensate for higher idiosyncratic risk. Net premium advantage should exceed additional risk.

universe_note: Trades AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, JPM â€” diversified basket of high-IV mega-caps
description: Call credit spread across AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, and JPM equally. 4% OTM strikes, 6% wide spreads. Diversification reduces single-stock event risk while maintaining above-SPY IV premium.
hypothesis: Spreading across 4 high-IV stocks reduces the impact of any single earnings/news event while capturing the structural IV premium that individual stocks carry over broad-market ETFs.

universe_note: Requires weekly expirations in cache â€” run: python main.py generate. Works best with the default top_50 tech-heavy universe.
description: 1-3 day directional long calls entered on RSI(14) < 35 oversold bounce. Target delta 0.60, DTE 7-21 days. Exit on 35% profit target, 20% stop loss, or 3-day max hold.
hypothesis: RSI oversold readings on quality tech stocks signal short-term mean-reversion snap-backs that weekly calls at 0.60 delta can capture before time decay erodes value.
management_rules: Take profit at +35% option value gain
Stop loss at -20% option value loss
Close after 3 calendar days regardless of P&L

universe_note: Requires weekly expirations in cache â€” run: python main.py generate. Works best with the default top_50 tech-heavy universe.
description: 1-3 day directional long calls entered when price breaks above the prior 5-day high (consolidation breakout). Target delta 0.65, DTE 7-21 days. Exit on 40% profit target, 22% stop, or 3-day hold.
hypothesis: Price breaking above recent consolidation on quality growth stocks signals continuation momentum. Weekly calls at 0.65 delta provide enough leverage to capture the initial breakout move in 1-3 days.
management_rules: Take profit at +40% option value gain
Stop loss at -22% option value loss
Close after 3 calendar days regardless of P&L

universe_note: Requires weekly expirations in cache â€” run: python main.py generate. Fewest signals â€” both RSI AND breakout required simultaneously.
description: High-conviction 1-2 day directional calls requiring BOTH RSI(14) < 40 AND a 5-day price breakout. Target delta 0.70, DTE 7-14 days. Exit on 50% profit, 25% stop, or 2-day hold.
hypothesis: Combining mean-reversion oversold signal with momentum breakout confirmation produces fewer but higher-conviction entries. Short DTE (7-14 days) maximises gamma exposure for quick large moves.
entry_rules: RSI(14) < 40 AND price > prior 5-day high (both required)
management_rules: Take profit at +50% option value gain
Stop loss at -25% option value loss
Close after 2 calendar days regardless of P&L

universe_note: Requires weekly expirations in cache â€” run: python main.py generate. Lower delta (0.55) means cheaper premium and lower capital at risk per trade.
description: Capital-preserving 1-3 day long calls on RSI(14) < 35 oversold. Target delta 0.55 (slightly OTM for lower cost), DTE 10-21 days. Tight 15% stop loss to protect capital.
hypothesis: Lower delta options cost less premium, so losses are smaller in absolute dollar terms. RSI-only signal on oversold quality stocks with a tight stop preserves capital while still capturing oversold bounces.
entry_rules: Target delta 0.55 (+/- 0.10) â€” slightly OTM for lower premium cost
management_rules: Take profit at +30% option value gain
Stop loss at -15% option value loss (tight â€” capital preservation focus)
Close after 3 calendar days regardless of P&L

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - switches spread direction by broad-trend regime rather than running bullish and bearish engines independently.
description: Regime-switch credit spread engine: legacy PCS profile in bullish regimes, CCS baseline profile in bearish or neutral regimes. Designed to reduce single-regime concentration while keeping the higher-quality spread templates already validated elsewhere in the repo.
hypothesis: Switching between put and call credit spreads by trend regime can preserve premium collection through more market states than a single-side spread engine.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - uses the stricter PCS VIX gate on bullish legs and the defensive CCS template on bearish or neutral legs.
description: Defensive regime-switch blend: PCS VIX-optimal on bullish regimes plus CCS defensive on bearish or neutral regimes, with a tighter overextension guard for short-call entries.
hypothesis: Combining stricter bullish PCS filters with a lower-delta bearish call-spread profile should improve drawdown behavior while retaining multi-regime premium capture.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - keeps the highest-return bullish PCS leg and swaps in the more defensive CCS template on bearish and neutral regimes.
description: Walk-forward validated all-weather performer. Combines the legacy PCS in bull markets with a defensive CCS in bear/neutral markets. Result: +189.4% return, OOS Sharpe 7.29, OOS max drawdown 1.14%.
hypothesis: Keeping the strongest bullish PCS edge while using the lower-delta CCS template on the call side may retain upside compounding and improve regime-switch robustness.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - uses the stricter bullish PCS VIX gate while keeping the higher-return CCS baseline for bearish and neutral regimes.
description: Volatility-aware regime-switch spread engine: PCS VIX-optimal in bullish regimes, CCS baseline in bearish and neutral regimes.
hypothesis: Filtering bullish PCS entries by volatility while preserving the higher-return baseline call-credit template may improve regime timing without sacrificing too much premium capture.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - legacy PCS in bullish regimes, defensive CCS in bearish regimes, and no call entries in neutral regimes.
description: Bear-only regime-switch hybrid: legacy PCS when trend is bullish, defensive CCS only in outright bearish regimes, and neutral periods held in cash.
hypothesis: Neutral regimes may be lower-conviction for short calls; sitting them out can reduce churn and keep regime-switching focused on clearer directional states.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - PCS VIX-optimal in bullish regimes, CCS baseline in bearish regimes, and no call entries in neutral regimes.
description: Bear-only volatility-aware regime switch: PCS VIX-optimal on bullish legs, CCS baseline only in outright bearish regimes, and neutral periods held in cash.
hypothesis: Using the stricter bullish PCS filter and skipping neutral call-selling may improve OOS discipline by concentrating trades in the most directional regimes.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - same legacy PCS plus defensive CCS regime router, but entries must also pass timed swing gates before opening a spread.
description: Timed legacy-defensive regime profile with bullish pullback and bearish/neutral rally-fade gates, 40% profit capture, 7 DTE exits, and 0.75x base risk sizing.
hypothesis: Reducing risk and harvesting profits faster may improve OOS quality if the main edge is entry timing rather than longer premium decay.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - timed entry version of the current winner with the same underlying leg templates and standard risk sizing.
description: Timed legacy-defensive regime profile using bullish pullback and bearish/neutral rally-fade gates, 40% profit capture, and 10 DTE exits.
hypothesis: The strongest regime router may improve if it waits for pullbacks and rally fades instead of opening spreads on every qualifying regime day.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - timed baseline version of the current regime winner.
description: Timed legacy-defensive baseline profile using pullback and rally-fade gates with 50% profit capture and 10 DTE exits.
hypothesis: Adding timing without changing the underlying spread templates may improve profit quality while preserving the existing regime winner's structure.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - timed entry version of the current winner that asks more from each spread before exiting.
description: Timed legacy-defensive regime profile using pullback and rally-fade gates with 60% profit capture and 10 DTE exits.
hypothesis: If the timed entries improve edge quality enough, holding for a larger share of credit may increase return without breaking the drawdown bar.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - timed entry version of the current winner with slower exits and larger per-trade risk.
description: Timed legacy-defensive regime profile using pullback and rally-fade gates with 50% profit capture, 14 DTE exits, and 1.25x risk sizing.
hypothesis: If timing filters reduce bad entries enough, a moderate size increase and slower exit may recover more of the premium opportunity.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - timed entry version of the current winner with neutral call entries disabled.
description: Timed legacy-defensive bear-only profile using bullish pullback gates for PCS, bearish rally-fade gates for CCS, no neutral call entries, 40% profit capture, and 10 DTE exits.
hypothesis: Neutral regimes may dilute the call-side edge; removing them while keeping timed bullish PCS entries could improve OOS discipline.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - bear-only timed baseline around the current regime winner.
description: Timed legacy-defensive bear-only baseline profile with 50% profit capture, 10 DTE exits, and no neutral call entries.
hypothesis: The strongest timed variant may come from keeping only clear bull and bear entries and ignoring neutral regimes entirely.

universe_note: Trades SPY + QQQ only - lower-risk bear-only timed variant with faster exits.
description: Timed legacy-defensive bear-only profile with 50% profit capture, 7 DTE exits, 0.75x risk sizing, and no neutral call entries.
hypothesis: If timed entries are strongest in clear bull and bear states, the lower-risk bear-only profile may offer the cleanest low-drawdown candidate.

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Softest score-gated version of the current winning legacy-PCS plus defensive-CCS router.
description: Score-gated regime credit spread with a 65-point threshold. Bullish sessions route into legacy PCS, bearish and neutral sessions route into defensive CCS, but the score gate is relaxed enough to preserve more entry frequency.
hypothesis: A 65-point floor may preserve the champion's breadth while still filtering out the weakest regime setups.

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Reuses the current winning legacy-PCS plus defensive-CCS router, but requires a state-aware setup score before entering.
description: Score-gated regime credit spread with a 70-point threshold. Bullish sessions route into legacy PCS, bearish and neutral sessions route into defensive CCS, but only when the active-side setup score clears the threshold.
hypothesis: A softer score floor may keep the current winner's trade frequency while filtering out the weakest regime days.

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Mid-threshold score-gated version of the current regime-credit leader.
description: Score-gated regime credit spread with a 75-point threshold. Uses the same legacy PCS plus defensive CCS router as the current leader with stricter entry selection.
hypothesis: A mid-strength score threshold may improve OOS quality without collapsing trade count.

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Highest-threshold score-gated version of the current regime-credit leader.
description: Score-gated regime credit spread with an 80-point threshold. Only the strongest setup-score days can open the legacy PCS or defensive CCS templates.
hypothesis: If weak entries are the real drag, the highest threshold should improve OOS quality even if return and trade count fall.

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Score-gated regime router with realized-volatility percentile sizing overlays.
description: Score-gated regime credit spread with a 75-point threshold plus volatility-targeted sizing. Risk scales down in high-volatility quartiles and up modestly in low-volatility aligned conditions.
hypothesis: Combining a strong setup score with volatility-aware sizing may improve compounding without breaking drawdown discipline.

description: Buy slightly ITM calls (delta ~0.55) on gap-up days when the stock closes >1.5% above the prior close. DTE 21-45 days. Target 80% premium gain within 5 days. Based on the gap-and-go momentum entry from the YouTube video strategy.
hypothesis: Stocks that close 1.5%+ above the prior close show institutional buying momentum likely to continue 1-5 days. ITM calls at ~0.55 delta give >50% probability of expiring in the money while controlling risk to the premium paid.

description: Buy slightly ITM puts (delta ~-0.55) on gap-down days when the stock closes >1.5% below the prior close. DTE 21-45 days. Target 80% premium gain within 5 days. The bearish mirror of the Gap Momentum Call strategy.
hypothesis: Stocks that close 1.5%+ below the prior close show institutional selling likely to continue 1-5 days. ITM puts at ~0.55 delta capture the leveraged downside with defined risk limited to premium paid.

universe_note: SPY only. Monthly 30-60 DTE iron-condor proxy because the local cache does not carry consistent 0DTE chains across the full backtest window.
description: Small-account SPY iron-condor research profile using one defined-risk monthly condor at a time with max risk capped below $1,000 per position.
hypothesis: A single small defined-risk SPY condor can preserve the capital-efficiency point from the transcript without pretending the repo has consistent 0DTE support.
entry_rules: Short put delta roughly -0.12 and short call delta roughly +0.12
Buy the next OTM wing on each side
Only enter when HV20 is moderate and trend is roughly neutral
management_rules: Take profit after 50% of credit
Stop if close cost reaches 2.0x entry credit
Close at 7 DTE
Close on short-strike breach

universe_note: MSFT only. Uses 30-60 DTE call spreads because the local cache does not provide enough true 1-year debit-spread opportunities to make the transcript example backtestable at scale.
description: Small-account MSFT bull call spread profile using one bullish debit spread at a time with net debit capped below $1,000 per trade.
hypothesis: A debit-capped MSFT bull call spread can express the transcript's capital-efficiency idea while staying inside liquid expirations that the cache supports consistently.
entry_rules: Short call delta roughly 0.30, same expiry and higher strike
Bullish trend filter (price above MA20 and MA20 above MA50)
management_rules: Take profit after capturing about 60% of max gain
Stop if spread value falls to roughly 55% of entry debit
Close at 14 DTE
Close on bearish trend break

universe_note: AAPL only. Uses real 38-52 DTE put spreads, a trailing IV percentile filter, and a forced 21-DTE exit to mirror the video's premium-selling framework on a liquid single name.
description: AAPL bull put spread profile inspired by the video's preference for selling expensive premium: target about 45 DTE, require elevated IV percentile, place the short put below recent support, and exit no later than 21 DTE.
hypothesis: Selling a defined-risk AAPL put spread only when implied volatility is expensive and the short strike sits below support should improve probability of profit versus buying naked calls.
entry_rules: Bullish trend filter: price above MA50 and MA200, MA20 above MA50
Short put delta roughly -0.27 inside a -0.40 to -0.15 range
Breakeven and short strike must both sit below the support reference
management_rules: Always exit by 21 DTE
Stop if close cost reaches 2.0x entry credit
Close on short-strike breach
Close on trend break below MA50

universe_note: AAPL only. This is the low-IV buying counterpart to the 45/21 premium-selling setup, using one 38-52 DTE long call at a time.
description: AAPL long call profile that buys premium only when IV percentile is historically cheap, with the same 45-DTE targeting and 21-DTE forced exit window used in the video's trade-management rule.
hypothesis: If the video's IV logic is directionally right, long calls should perform better when volatility is cheap, while the bull put spread should dominate when volatility is expensive.
entry_rules: Bullish trend filter: price above MA20, MA50, and MA200 with MA20 above MA50
Long call delta roughly 0.60 inside a 0.45 to 0.75 range
management_rules: Always exit by 21 DTE
Stop if option value falls to about 55% of entry debit
Close on bearish trend break

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Research-first hybrid that switches between buying premium and selling premium based on regime and IV, while keeping the official dashboard metrics separate from any later 10-year extension.
description: Baseline 30-45 DTE index swing profile. In bullish pullbacks with low IV it buys a bull call debit spread; in bullish pullbacks with richer IV it sells a put credit spread; in bearish or neutral rally-fade setups with high IV it sells a call credit spread.
hypothesis: SPY and QQQ swing entries should behave better when the structure matches the volatility regime instead of forcing long calls or credit spreads in every environment.
entry_rules: Bull regime: close above MA200, MA20 above MA50, and close above MA50
Bull pullback trigger: 3-day return between -4.0% and -0.75% plus RSI14 <= 48
Low IV percentile (< 40) routes to a 30-45 DTE bull call debit spread
Higher IV percentile (>= 40) routes to the legacy PCS template
Bear or neutral rally-fade trigger with IV percentile >= 55 routes to the CCS baseline template
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 50% of max gain, stop at 35% loss of debit, close at 10 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits legacy_replica management
CCS leg inherits ccs_baseline management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Defensive 30-45 DTE version with lower risk per position and tighter debit-spread exits.
description: Defensive 30-45 DTE hybrid swing profile. Uses the same regime router as the baseline variant but swaps in pcs_vix_optimal and ccs_defensive while tightening the debit-spread exit rules.
hypothesis: A defensive hybrid that buys less extrinsic value and leans on stricter credit-spread templates may improve OOS stability even if raw return falls.
entry_rules: Same bull pullback and bear/neutral rally-fade triggers as the baseline profile
Low IV percentile (< 40) routes to a 30-45 DTE bull call debit spread capped at $900
Higher IV percentile (>= 40) routes to pcs_vix_optimal
Bear or neutral rally-fade setup with IV percentile >= 55 routes to ccs_defensive
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 40% of max gain, stop at 25% loss of debit, close at 14 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits pcs_vix_optimal management
CCS leg inherits ccs_defensive management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Extends the low-IV debit-spread leg into a 45-60 DTE bucket while keeping the same hybrid routing logic.
description: Baseline 45-60 DTE hybrid swing profile. Same regime and IV router as the 30-45 variant, but with a slower 45-60 DTE bull call spread to test whether a longer holding envelope matches the swing signal better.
hypothesis: A longer debit-spread DTE bucket may better fit multi-day index swing signals while preserving the same premium-selling branches in high-IV environments.
entry_rules: Same bull pullback and bear/neutral rally-fade triggers as the baseline profile
Low IV percentile (< 40) routes to a 45-60 DTE bull call debit spread
Higher IV percentile (>= 40) routes to the legacy PCS template
Bear or neutral rally-fade setup with IV percentile >= 55 routes to the CCS baseline template
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 50% of max gain, stop at 35% loss of debit, close at 10 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits legacy_replica management
CCS leg inherits ccs_baseline management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Defensive 45-60 DTE hybrid with lower risk per position and the stricter PCS/CCS templates.
description: Defensive 45-60 DTE hybrid swing profile. Keeps the longer debit-spread bucket but uses pcs_vix_optimal, ccs_defensive, and tighter debit-spread exits.
hypothesis: If longer-duration debit spreads are viable, the defensive structure mix should be the cleanest first candidate for OOS survival.
entry_rules: Same bull pullback and bear/neutral rally-fade triggers as the other family variants
Low IV percentile (< 40) routes to a 45-60 DTE bull call debit spread capped at $900
Higher IV percentile (>= 40) routes to pcs_vix_optimal
Bear or neutral rally-fade setup with IV percentile >= 55 routes to ccs_defensive
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 40% of max gain, stop at 25% loss of debit, close at 14 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits pcs_vix_optimal management
CCS leg inherits ccs_defensive management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Second-sweep 30-45 DTE hybrid that broadens the trigger envelope so the high-IV PCS and CCS branches can actually fire more often.
description: Baseline 30-45 DTE index swing profile, v2. Broadens the bull pullback and rally-fade triggers, lowers the low-IV cutoff to 25, and allows an overextended bullish tape to route into the CCS baseline template when volatility is elevated.
hypothesis: The first sweep was too sparse and over-weighted the low-IV debit-spread branch. A broader trigger window should make the hybrid behave like a real structure router instead of a thin debit-spread strategy.
entry_rules: Bull pullback trigger widened to 3-day return between -6.0% and -0.5% with RSI14 <= 52
Low IV percentile (< 25) routes to a 30-45 DTE bull call debit spread
Bull pullbacks with IV percentile >= 25 route to the legacy PCS template
Bear or neutral rally-fade setups with IV percentile >= 45 route to the CCS baseline template
Overextended bullish rallies may also route to CCS baseline when IV percentile >= 60
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 50% of max gain, stop at 35% loss of debit, close at 10 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits legacy_replica management
CCS leg inherits ccs_baseline management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Defensive 30-45 DTE second sweep with broader routing thresholds, smaller debit caps, and defensive PCS/CCS templates.
description: Defensive 30-45 DTE hybrid swing profile, v2. Uses the broader second-sweep trigger envelope, routes higher-IV bullish pullbacks into pcs_vix_optimal, and uses ccs_defensive for rally-fade shorts.
hypothesis: If the first sweep was missing too many valid high-IV setups, broadening the signals while keeping the defensive subtemplates should improve coverage without giving up all discipline.
entry_rules: Same widened pullback and rally-fade triggers as the baseline v2 profile
Low IV percentile (< 25) routes to a 30-45 DTE bull call debit spread capped at $900
Bull pullbacks with IV percentile >= 25 route to pcs_vix_optimal
Bear or neutral rally-fade setups with IV percentile >= 45 route to ccs_defensive
Overextended bullish rallies may also route to ccs_defensive when IV percentile >= 55
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 40% of max gain, stop at 25% loss of debit, close at 14 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits pcs_vix_optimal management
CCS leg inherits ccs_defensive management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Second-sweep 45-60 DTE hybrid that broadens the trigger envelope and keeps a slower debit-spread bucket.
description: Baseline 45-60 DTE index swing profile, v2. Uses the broader second-sweep routing thresholds while keeping the longer 45-60 DTE debit-spread branch and the baseline PCS/CCS subtemplates.
hypothesis: If the swing signal has a longer half-life, the broader hybrid router should work better with a slower debit-spread bucket and more frequent high-IV routing.
entry_rules: Bull pullback trigger widened to 3-day return between -6.0% and -0.5% with RSI14 <= 52
Low IV percentile (< 25) routes to a 45-60 DTE bull call debit spread
Bull pullbacks with IV percentile >= 25 route to the legacy PCS template
Bear or neutral rally-fade setups with IV percentile >= 45 route to the CCS baseline template
Overextended bullish rallies may also route to CCS baseline when IV percentile >= 60
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 50% of max gain, stop at 35% loss of debit, close at 10 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits legacy_replica management
CCS leg inherits ccs_baseline management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Defensive 45-60 DTE second sweep with broader triggers and defensive PCS/CCS routing.
description: Defensive 45-60 DTE hybrid swing profile, v2. Uses the broader second-sweep trigger envelope, a $900 debit cap, pcs_vix_optimal, and ccs_defensive while keeping the longer debit bucket.
hypothesis: A broader signal envelope plus the longer debit bucket may improve trade count, but the defensive structure mix still needs to keep drawdowns controlled.
entry_rules: Same widened pullback and rally-fade triggers as the baseline v2 profile
Low IV percentile (< 25) routes to a 45-60 DTE bull call debit spread capped at $900
Bull pullbacks with IV percentile >= 25 route to pcs_vix_optimal
Bear or neutral rally-fade setups with IV percentile >= 45 route to ccs_defensive
Overextended bullish rallies may also route to ccs_defensive when IV percentile >= 55
management_rules: Bull call spread: take profit at 40% of max gain, stop at 25% loss of debit, close at 14 DTE, close on bearish trend break
PCS leg inherits pcs_vix_optimal management
CCS leg inherits ccs_defensive management
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions

universe_note: SPY and QQQ only. Third-generation hybrid router designed to produce a more balanced mix of debit and credit spreads across volatility regimes.
description: Third-generation index swing hybrid. Refines the v2 routing logic by creating explicit volatility bands for debit spreads (low IV), standard credit spreads (mid IV), and defensive credit spreads (high IV) to improve structural selection.
hypothesis: The v2 variants skewed heavily toward debit spreads. By creating explicit IV tiers for different structures (e.g., Bull Call for IVpct<25, legacy PCS for IVpct 25-60, defensive PCS for IVpct>60), the router should achieve a more balanced trade distribution and better all-weather performance.
entry_rules: Bull Pullback Trigger: 3-day return between -6.0% and -0.75% with RSI14 <= 50 in a bull regime
IF IV percentile < 25, routes to 30-45 DTE Bull Call Spread
IF IV percentile is 25-60, routes to legacy_replica PCS template
IF IV percentile > 60, routes to pcs_vix_optimal (defensive) PCS template
Bear/Neutral Rally-Fade Trigger: 3-day return > 1.0% and RSI14 > 55 in a non-bull regime
IF IV percentile >= 45, routes to ccs_baseline CCS template
IF IV percentile >= 65, routes to ccs_defensive CCS template
management_rules: Each leg inherits the management rules of its underlying template (Bull Call Spread, PCS, CCS).
One position per symbol, max 2 concurrent positions.

universe_note: QQQ only. Low-IV bullish pullback research variant using real QQQ option-chain selection in a 30-45 DTE debit-spread bucket.
description: QQQ convex swing pullback profile. In a bullish regime with breadth support and low IV percentile, it buys a 30-45 DTE bull call debit spread after a controlled 3-day pullback.
hypothesis: If the repo needs a convex upside sleeve at all, QQQ low-IV pullbacks are the cleanest place to start rather than forcing premium-buying into every regime.
entry_rules: Bull regime: close > MA200, MA20 > MA50, breadth >= 55, IV percentile < 35
Bull call debit spread with long delta near 0.60 and short delta near 0.30
management_rules: Take profit at 50% of max gain
Stop at 35% loss of debit
Time exit at 10 DTE
Close on bearish trend break

universe_note: QQQ only. Low-IV breakout research variant using real QQQ option-chain selection in a 30-45 DTE debit-spread bucket.
description: QQQ convex swing breakout profile. In a bullish regime with supportive breadth and low IV percentile, it buys a 30-45 DTE bull call debit spread on fresh 20-day breakouts.
hypothesis: A low-IV breakout may offer a better convex upside profile than pullbacks if the strongest QQQ trends keep extending after new highs.
entry_rules: Bull regime: close > MA200, MA20 > MA50, breadth >= 55, IV percentile < 35
management_rules: Take profit at 50% of max gain
Stop at 35% loss of debit
Time exit at 10 DTE
Close on bearish trend break

universe_note: TQQQ only. Intended to use real TQQQ option-chain selection in a 21-35 DTE debit-spread bucket, but local cache coverage may be incomplete.
description: TQQQ convex swing pullback profile. In a bullish regime with breadth support and low IV percentile, it looks for a 21-35 DTE bull call debit spread after a controlled pullback.
hypothesis: If convex upside belongs anywhere in this repo, a faster TQQQ pullback sleeve may provide the highest upside per trade, but only if real chain coverage is adequate.

universe_note: TQQQ only. Intended to use real TQQQ option-chain selection in a 21-35 DTE debit-spread bucket, but local cache coverage may be incomplete.
description: TQQQ convex swing breakout profile. In a bullish regime with breadth support and low IV percentile, it looks for a 21-35 DTE bull call debit spread on fresh breakouts.
hypothesis: A leveraged ETF breakout sleeve could produce the biggest raw upside, but only if the local data can support realistic entries and exits.

universe_note: Top-50 universe. Long-dated deep ITM call proxy using the longest liquid DTE bucket present in the local cache rather than true 365+ LEAPS.
description: Small-account long-dated ITM call profile: one contract max, one position max, contract cost capped at $1,000, 120-200 DTE, deep ITM and low-extrinsic only.
hypothesis: Capping contract cost while keeping high-delta, low-extrinsic long calls may preserve stock-replacement behavior in a form that a small account can actually fund.

universe_note: Designed for the default or top_50 stock-replacement universes. The local cache does not include open-interest values, so the 250-OI rule is encoded but cannot be enforced historically; liquidity is instead constrained through spread caps.
description: Short-DTE deep ITM stock replacement profile based on the transcript's high-delta call framework: 0.80 delta target, max 30% extrinsic, Stage-2 trend gate, and a hard $0.50 bid/ask spread cap.
hypothesis: Using shorter-dated deep ITM calls with tight spread control and Stage-2 trend confirmation should increase capital efficiency without paying the full LEAPS time premium.
entry_rules: Bid/ask spread <= 12% of ask and <= $0.50 absolute
Bullish Stage-2 trend: 10 EMA > 20 EMA and price > 50 EMA
management_rules: Exit on bearish 10/20 EMA cross
Roll when delta falls below the minimum threshold
Roll during the final week before expiration

universe_note: Designed for the default or top_50 stock-replacement universes. Uses the same absolute spread cap as ditm_stage2_liquid, but only enters when both the symbol and SPY regime are bullish.
description: 30-60 DTE deep ITM stock replacement variant that keeps the Stage-2 trend discipline, extends the holding window, and sits in cash when SPY is bearish.
hypothesis: A longer DTE window plus an SPY cash-regime gate should reduce whipsaw and improve out-of-sample stability versus the shorter 14-28 DTE DITM profile.
entry_rules: Bid/ask spread <= 10% of ask and <= $0.50 absolute
Bullish Stage-2 trend: 10 EMA > 20 EMA and price > 50 EMA
SPY must also be in bullish 10/20/50 EMA trend
management_rules: Exit on bearish 10/20 EMA cross
Roll when delta falls below the minimum threshold
Roll during the final week before expiration
Sit in cash while SPY regime is bearish
